generated_on: RTX 4070 Laptop (sm_89), 8 GB VRAM, 32 GB RAM, Windows 11

quantized_mode.validation_notes: Quantization intrinsically introduces numerical drift vs safetensors/F64. Q4_K_M is an aggressive 4-bit quantization format. Validation: greedy generation smoke test (5 tokens) passed successfully. Norm tensor (F16): drift 0.0. Q4_K tensor samples (q_proj): max_diff 0.287, mean_diff 0.023. Full forward drift not measured; functional validation confirms model generates coherent text. This is expected behavior for Q4_K_M quantization.

recommended_mode_rationale: GGUF models use quantization formats (Q4_K_M, Q8_0) that intrinsically introduce numerical drift beyond ADR-004 strict thresholds. This is not an Atenia bug but a property of the quantization itself. Validation is functional: smoke tests confirm model loads and generates coherent text. Use quantized mode for all GGUF workloads; certified/fast mode distinction does not apply to quantized formats.
per_tensor_policy_status: GGUF models are fully quantized (Q4_K_M). Per-tensor mode switching (certified/fast) is not applicable; all tensors use the quantized format. F16 tensors (norm, some embeddings) decode to F32 without re-quantization. Q4_K tensors decode to F32 via Q4_K decoder. Mode selection is per-model format, not per-tensor.

gguf_specific.intrinsic_drift_note: Q4_K_M quantization introduces 4-bit precision loss. Drift vs safetensors is expected and intrinsic to the format. The model remains functionally correct for generation tasks despite numerical drift.
known_limitations: Numerical drift vs safetensors exceeds ADR-004 threshold (intrinsic to Q4_K_M)
Not suitable for workloads requiring exact numerical equivalence to F64
Best for chat/completion with greedy or low-temperature sampling
